entities.name: Moonshot
entities.context: High-risk endeavor that forces operation at elevated complexity levels, creating permanent capability expansion even if it fails financially

entities.name: Slam Dunk
entities.context: Low-risk endeavor with high win probability but limited growth potential due to lower complexity requirements

entities.name: Ratchet Effect
entities.context: Mechanism where solving problems at moonshot scale permanently elevates baseline capacity - once achieved, cannot regress

entities.name: Complexity Exposure
entities.context: Strategy of occasionally optimizing for exposure to hard problems over win probability for long-term capability compounding

entities.name: Capability Compounding
entities.context: Long-term accumulation of skills and perspectives through non-linear, cumulative growth from challenging endeavors

relationships.context: Pursuing moonshots triggers the ratchet effect by forcing operation at higher complexity levels
Even failed moonshots contribute to long-term capability compounding through complexity exposure
Success on easy tasks provides inferior investment in capability growth compared to hard task failures
Optimizing for complexity exposure drives long-term compounding of capability over short-term wins
The ratchet effect ensures that capability gains from hard problems are permanent and cumulative
